integration_blocked_until: operator posts HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN in monitor/decisions/human-notes.json with approved_exp_ids containing 'EXP-210'. Ordering constraint: EXP-202 (tokens) MUST land before or with EXP-210; this EXP re-routes the --refuted / --refuted-solid verdict tokens through --semantic-crit / --semantic-crit-soft introduced in EXP-202. EXP-207 (EXP-A2 typography application) and EXP-210 are independent and may integrate in either order, though landing A2 first lets A5's surviving red appear on top of the final type roles (recommended).
target: build-scripts/generate-html.js (CSS :root / @media dark / @media print token blocks and four inline border-left:4px solid #c45050 style literals in Overview/stat-card templates and one helper function that emits FAIL-NNN entry cards); data/sections.json (part1.html stat-card border-lefts with #c45050 + #8b0000; part4.html stat-card border-left with #c45050; part5.html kill-shot 'Refuted by Data' and 'Decisive' inline pill-badges with #B71C1C / #FFCCCC / #FFCDD2 / #EF9A9A chroma literals; one prose-inline [SUSPENDED] span with color:#8b0000 in docs/index.html via generate-html.js); test.js (two new assertions verifying raw red hex literal count is 0 for #c45050, #8b0000, #B71C1C, #EF9A9A, #FFCCCC, #FFCDD2 outside of verdict-token declarations, and that --refuted and --refuted-solid resolve to semantic-crit tokens). docs/index.html is a build artifact; the actual source edits live in generate-html.js + sections.json.
scope_item: Item 7 application — restrict red to actual in-table contradictions (EXP-A5 in the brief's suggested sequence). Re-routes --refuted / --refuted-solid through EXP-202's --semantic-crit / --semantic-crit-soft tokens so verdict pills keep a restrained red; neutralizes four stat-card border-left accents (#c45050, #8b0000) to var(--rule); converts FAIL-NNN silent-failure entry cards to var(--rule); migrates kill-shot inline 'Refuted by Data' / 'Decisive' badges to route through --semantic-crit tokens (judgment call flagged for the human group — see notes); migrates .ca-tag.tag-monitoring-hardcoded to --semantic-warn (amber) because its semantic is 'warning', not 'refuted'. Preserves the 'Kill Shots' label and tab styling unchanged.
source: HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001 section 'SUGGESTED EXP ORDERING' step EXP-A5; item enumerated under SCOPE Item 7 (restrict red to actual in-table contradictions). Builds on EXP-202 (tokens).
kernel_of_truth: The external reviewer is right that our current palette uses red as a mood signal for entire surfaces before the reader has read a sentence. Three concrete cases ground the critique: (1) the 4+4 Actual-Failures stat card on the Overview carries a #c45050 border-left that pre-loads the verdict; (2) the Silent-Failures stat card carries an #8b0000 border-left that is even more saturated; (3) seven FAIL-NNN entry cards in the silent-failures section each carry the same #c45050 border-left, so the reader sees a vertical red bar pattern before reading the content. A debunking document that is careful about evidence in its prose but signals 'RED ALERT' in its chrome is slightly self-undermining — the chrome is telling the reader what to think before the evidence does. Neutralizing the chrome does not weaken the argument; it strengthens credibility. The reviewer is also right that color-coded verdicts without an explicit label regression hazard color-blind readers (Item 3 a11y), so ANY red that survives should pair with a glyph+label. What the reviewer gets wrong is treating the 'Refuted by Data' kill-shot badges as chrome — those are the textual equivalent of a <td> verdict pill in a table, marking a specific row outcome, and they earn their red the same way. This EXP keeps the text-contradiction reds (td.v-refuted, kill-shot refuted badges) and removes the ambient-chrome reds (stat-card borders, entry-card borders, prose [SUSPENDED] ink color).

content_diff.description: Four coordinated changes. (1) Re-route --refuted and --refuted-solid through --semantic-crit-soft / --semantic-crit in generate-html.js :root, @media dark, and @media print token blocks — no selector changes, token values become var() aliases. (2) Replace border-left:4px solid #c45050 and #8b0000 at four source locations in generate-html.js (Overview stat cards + FAIL-NNN helper) and three source locations in sections.json (part1 two stat cards, part4 one stat card) with border-left:4px solid var(--rule). (3) Migrate kill-shot 'Refuted by Data' / 'Decisive' inline badges in sections.json part5.html from raw #FFCCCC/#B71C1C/#FFCDD2/#EF9A9A hex to var(--semantic-crit-soft) / var(--semantic-crit) / var(--semantic-crit-soft) / var(--semantic-crit). Text labels and glyph-readiness preserved; Item 3 (a11y) will add the glyph pairing in EXP-A8. (4) Migrate .ca-tag.tag-monitoring-hardcoded in generate-html.js CSS block from #FFEBEE / #B71C1C / #EF9A9A to var(--semantic-warn-soft) / var(--semantic-warn) / var(--semantic-warn) — because the 'hardcoded monitoring' semantic is a warning (look-here-the-number-is-stale), not a refutation. Also migrate the one inline [SUSPENDED] span color:#8b0000 in generate-html.js to color:var(--ink-3);font-weight:600 — prose-ink neutral. No other selectors touched. --refuted verdict pill backgrounds and td.v-refuted cells continue to render red via the re-routed token, so the surviving red is in verdict pills and table cells only.

change_1_token_rerouting.where: build-scripts/generate-html.js inside the const CSS block, three places: :root declaration (around line 434), @media(prefers-color-scheme:dark):root declaration (around line 435), and @media print :root declaration (near the bottom of the CSS block).
change_1_token_rerouting.rationale: The existing --refuted / --refuted-solid tokens are consumed by many selectors (td.v-refuted, .vt-refuted, .vl-refuted, .ks-status.ks-claimed, and the pie-chart slice). Changing the token values to route through --semantic-crit-soft / --semantic-crit (introduced by EXP-202) keeps every consumer intact while tying the surviving red to the same source-of-truth the rest of Phase A uses. The re-routed values are slightly less saturated than the current hex values (dark --semantic-crit = #d46a6a vs. current --refuted-solid = #ef5350), which is exactly the brief's restraint direction.

root_light.note_light_background_ambient: In the light-theme root, --semantic-crit-soft = #f2dada — a warm muted pink. The previous rgba(229,115,115,0.25) also renders as a warm pink over white. Visual delta is ~5%. Acceptable; print pathway preserved.

root_dark.note_dark_background_ambient: In the dark-theme override, --semantic-crit-soft = #3d1f1f (deep muted red-brown) vs. previous rgba(239,83,80,0.2) over #1a1a2e which computes to approximately #332624. The new value is slightly more chromatic in the red direction but visually similar; crucially, both pass WCAG AA when paired with the new --semantic-crit = #d46a6a ink (which EXP-A8 will verify against the formal audit).

root_print.to: identical to root_light values — print override mirrors light theme, and the var() aliases resolve correctly in print rendering.
root_print.note: No additional work; the print @media block already mirrors the light :root declaration structure.

generate_html_js_locations.context: Helper function that emits FAIL-NNN entry cards (silent-failure list).
Part3 small stat-card for Acknowledged Failures (mini summary in part3 header).
Overview hero stat card for 4+4 Actual Failures.
Part3 header stat card — Acknowledged Failures (expanded).

sections_json_locations.context: Part1 Overview stat-card — Silent Failures. The darker #8b0000 hex goes away too; it is even more saturated than #c45050.
Part4 stat card — Independent Numeric Failures (12).

change_2_stat_card_border_neutralization.rationale: All four generate-html.js locations and three sections.json locations are overview/summary stat cards. None is inside a <td>; none is a verdict pill. Per Item 7, the red chrome comes off. Neutralizing to var(--rule) — EXP-202's generic divider/rule token — keeps the border as a structural edge while dropping the chromatic alarm. The sc-number inside retains its existing weight and size (the brief's explicit preservation: 'retain whatever weight/size they had').

change_3_kill_shot_badge_migration.where: data/sections.json part5.html — the three kill-shot <h2> headings in the Kill Shots tab each carry two inline pill-badges ('Refuted by Data' and 'Decisive') with raw hex styling. Six substring replacements total, affecting three kill-shot headings.
change_3_kill_shot_badge_migration.rationale: These badges are the prose equivalent of a <td class='v-refuted'> verdict pill — they mark a specific kill-shot as refuted and specific to the testability axis. The brief's stated rule is 'Red survives only inside table cells that flag an actual contradiction'. Strict reading would kill these too. Judgment call: these badges ARE contradiction markers attached to a specific row-like heading (each kill-shot is a 'row' in the argumentative sense). Keeping them red — but migrating the raw hex to var(--semantic-crit-soft) / var(--semantic-crit) — binds them to the same source-of-truth that td.v-refuted uses. If the human review group prefers the strict reading, the migration to var()-routed colors makes the subsequent 'neutralize the kill-shot badges too' flip a one-line diff.

patches.context: Kill shot 1 heading — Refuted by Data badge

patches.note: Same from/to applies to all three Decisive badges in part5.html. The background/color/border triple moves as a unit.

patches.context: Kill shot 2 and 3 — Refuted by Data second-badge variants with margin-left
patches.note: These appear on kill-shots 2 and 3; same migration.

change_3_kill_shot_badge_migration.verification: After migration, grep -c '#FFCCCC\|#B71C1C\|#EF9A9A\|#FFCDD2' data/sections.json should return 0 (all raw chroma gone, all badges routed through tokens).

ca_tag_monitoring_hardcoded.rationale: The 'hardcoded monitoring' tag warns the reader that a specific claim is pinned to a hardcoded number the dome has not updated. Semantically this is a WARNING, not a refutation. Routing through --semantic-warn (dark: #d99860 amber / soft: #3d2e20) matches semantics to color. Item 3 (a11y) will add the glyph pair in EXP-A8.

suspended_inline_span.rationale: Single prose-inline occurrence marking a suspended WIN row. This is not an in-table contradiction; it is a prose tag. Neutralize to --ink-3 (faint-prose) so the [SUSPENDED] label reads as a quiet annotation rather than an alarm. The visual weight of font-weight:600 plus the bracket notation does the emphasis job without chroma.

change_5_no_changes_to: Kill Shots tab button — .tab-btn rendering is already neutral (uses --accent blue, no red). No change needed per the brief: Kill Shots label and tab styling preserved.

Pie-chart slice fills (#E57373 light / #ef5350 dark) — these are data viz in the overview pie chart. They encode verdict data, not chrome; the refuted slice legitimately reads as refuted. The --refuted-solid token re-routing means these slice fills remain tied to --semantic-crit on the next refactor when the pie is recomputed at build time. For now the hex values stay because the pie SVG was generated with baked-in color attributes; a future EXP (not Phase A scope) should re-render the pie with var() fill attributes. Tracked in notes_for_human_review_group.

td.v-refuted / .vt-refuted / .vl-refuted selectors — these are verdict table cells and pill labels inside verdict tables. They consume --refuted / --refuted-solid, which now resolves via --semantic-crit. No selector change; the cells stay visually refuted.
Existing .vl-misleading, .vt-misleading (orange) and other non-red verdict tokens — untouched. This EXP is about red only.
Segoe UI body font-family, .formula class, Google Fonts — EXP-A2 scope, not here.
Masthead stance statement and hero thesis — EXP-A3 scope.
Verdict distribution bar chart — EXP-A4 scope.
Latest Findings visual demotion — EXP-A7 scope.
Glyph + label pairing for verdict pills — EXP-A8 scope. This EXP assumes A8 will add the glyphs.

acceptance_criteria: grep -c "#c45050" docs/index.html returns 0 (or only emits counts inside comments, if any — none exist in current source).
grep -c "#B71C1C" docs/index.html returns 0 except inside the pie-chart SVG block (pie fills are out of scope for this EXP; track as a later Phase B cleanup).
grep -c "#FFCCCC\|#FFCDD2\|#EF9A9A\|#FFEBEE" docs/index.html returns 0 except inside the pie-chart SVG.
DevTools Computed panel on a td.v-refuted shows background-color resolved from var(--semantic-crit-soft) and color/border from var(--semantic-crit).
Overview stat cards (Actual Failures 4+4, Acknowledged Failures, Silent Failures, Part4 Independent Numeric Failures) render with a var(--rule) border-left — no red border visible.
Kill-shot headings in part5 render "Refuted by Data" / "Decisive" badges in var(--semantic-crit) chroma — still red, now tokenized.
.ca-tag.tag-monitoring-hardcoded renders as an amber/warn pill, not a red pill.
The single [SUSPENDED] inline span renders in --ink-3 neutral ink.

test.js passes with two new assertions: (a) raw red hex literal count is 0 for #c45050/#8b0000/#B71C1C/#EF9A9A/#FFCCCC/#FFCDD2/#FFEBEE outside the pie SVG; (b) the --refuted and --refuted-solid tokens in the CSS block reference var(--semantic-crit-soft) and var(--semantic-crit) respectively.
Visual regression: Kill Shots tab button styling unchanged; td.v-refuted cells still read as refuted-colored; pie chart untouched.
Build reproducibility: fresh clone + node build.js html produces byte-identical docs/index.html (up to the embedded date line).

Brief acceptance — page-wide color-usage audit (post-integration): red pixels account for under 5% of total colored pixels, and every remaining red instance resolves to a verdict pill or table cell (not chrome).

anticipated_objections.objection: Re-routing --refuted through --semantic-crit changes the visual red. Some readers who remember the previous red will notice the shift.
anticipated_objections.response: True but small. Dark-theme: previous rgba(239,83,80,0.2) over #1a1a2e background renders as approximately #332624; new --semantic-crit-soft = #3d1f1f is slightly more chromatic. The difference is roughly JND (just-noticeable-difference) for trained eyes and invisible to most readers. The behavioral effect — that 'refuted' still reads as red — is preserved. Rejection path: if the human review group prefers bit-for-bit color preservation, keep --refuted's raw value and only re-route --refuted-solid for the chroma tokens; this EXP can be amended in a post-curmudgeon revision.

anticipated_objections.objection: The kill-shot 'Refuted by Data' / 'Decisive' badges are NOT in <td> cells. Strict reading of Item 7 says neutralize them, not keep them red.
anticipated_objections.response: Flagged explicitly in notes_for_human_review_group. The analyst's judgment call is: these badges are prose-inline verdict pills attached to kill-shot headings, and each kill-shot is a 'row' in the Kill Shots argumentative table even if the HTML wrapper is <h2> and not <tr>. They mark specific row-level outcomes. The migration to var(--semantic-crit-soft) / var(--semantic-crit) ties them to the same token as td.v-refuted, so if the group decides to neutralize them too, the flip is a single global swap — replace var(--semantic-crit) with var(--ink-2) across part5.html badges — not a re-research. Pre-staging the tokenization keeps the later cleanup cheap.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why migrate .ca-tag.tag-monitoring-hardcoded to --semantic-warn (amber) instead of neutralizing entirely?
anticipated_objections.response: Because the tag conveys semantic information the reader needs: 'this specific claim is hardcoded in monitor.py rather than pulled from live data.' Neutralizing to --ink-3 would fold it into the visual noise of other .ca-tag variants (tag-true, tag-false, tag-pending) — the semantic marker would fail its job. Amber/warn chroma keeps the visual distinction, costs no red pixels, and matches the 'this-is-a-warning-not-a-refutation' meaning. The three other ca-tag variants (tag-monitoring-live, tag-monitoring-none, tag-pending) are not touched by this EXP and retain their current chroma — those are not red and are already semantically correct.

anticipated_objections.objection: The pie chart still has raw #E57373 / #ef5350 / #B71C1C color literals baked into the SVG. This EXP claims to restrict red but leaves the most visible red on the page untouched.
anticipated_objections.response: Acknowledged. The pie-chart SVG was pre-rendered with literal fills via an offline script and is embedded in docs/index.html as static SVG. Re-rendering it to consume var() fills requires touching generate-html.js's pie-chart emitter (if that's where it comes from) or the upstream data-viz script. Scope-of-work-wise this is Phase B territory: the chart's red IS a verdict-encoded data color (the refuted slice IS refuted), so semantically it earns its red in the same way td.v-refuted does. Tracking in notes_for_human_review_group for a later EXP. If the group wants this in Phase A, a follow-up EXP-A5b can ship, but landing it in A5 triples the review surface and is not worth the complexity.

anticipated_objections.objection: Privacy regression check: moving to var() references doesn't change privacy posture (no new network requests from this EXP). But does it break the print pathway?
anticipated_objections.response: No. The @media print :root block already inherits from the light root structure. After this EXP, print output still has --refuted = var(--semantic-crit-soft) with --semantic-crit-soft set in the print :root. A test print via 'node build.js pdf' verifies: the print PDF renders td.v-refuted cells in the same warm muted pink the light theme renders. PDF acceptance criterion added.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why neutralize stat-card border-lefts but keep the border itself? Wouldn't no-border-at-all be more restrained?
anticipated_objections.response: The border-left is structural — it visually separates a stat card from body prose and from other sc-cards. Removing the border loses that affordance. Neutralizing the color to var(--rule) keeps the structural edge without the chromatic alarm. If the human review group prefers a no-border variant, the edit is one line in .sc-card CSS. This EXP's default is 'keep the structure, lose the chroma' because the brief's language is 'lose the red color; retain whatever weight/size they had' — i.e., preserve structural weight.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: 'Kill Shots' is not chrome
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: Kill Shots tab label unchanged. Tab button CSS unchanged (already neutral, uses --accent blue). Kill-shot <h2> headings on the Kill Shots tab retain their 'Refuted by Data' / 'Decisive' badges in var(--semantic-crit) chroma — semantic force preserved, chrome weight reduced by token rerouting. The word 'Kill Shots' remains in the tab and section labels.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: 4+4 failure accounting is calibration content
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: The 4+4 Actual-Failures stat card on the Overview keeps its sc-number and sc-label text; only the border-left color changes from #c45050 to var(--rule). The arithmetic and link to detail (#p3-failures) are untouched. EXP-A6 will demote this card to a sidebar; this EXP does not prejudge that placement — the card stays on the Overview with neutralized chrome until A6 moves it.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: Latest Findings is evidence, not ticker chrome
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: Not touched by this EXP. EXP-A7 owns Latest Findings visual treatment.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: Dark theme is differentiator, not oversight
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: All token re-routings define dark-theme overrides. --semantic-crit = #d46a6a dark / #7a2e2e light; --semantic-crit-soft = #3d1f1f dark / #f2dada light. The surviving red in verdict pills reads as a muted restrained red against the deep navy #1a1a2e background — on paper (light/print) it reads as a warm pink against white. NC1 honored — no paper-theme aesthetic imported.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: Hero thesis candidate is a CANDIDATE
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: Not touched by this EXP. Masthead content is EXP-A3 scope.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: Verdict distribution chart must route through generate-html.js computed counts
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: Not touched by this EXP. EXP-A4 owns the bar chart insertion.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: Stance statement is operator-authored
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: Not touched by this EXP. EXP-A3 owns the stance statement.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Dark theme stays
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: All token re-routings have dark overrides. Surviving red = --semantic-crit on dark = #d46a6a, which is dark-theme-calibrated. No paper aesthetic adopted.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Tabs stay
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: No IA change; no tab CSS touched. Kill Shots tab label and button styling unchanged.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: 'Kill Shots' term stays
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: Tab label unchanged. Kill-shot headings unchanged except for inline-badge chroma migration (still red, now tokenized).

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Computed counts rule
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: No numeric claims introduced. Color token re-routing contains no counts.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Build reproducibility
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: All changes are static CSS token re-routing and substring replacements. Fresh build produces byte-identical output. Acceptance criterion requires this.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Tests green
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: Two new assertions added to test.js (red hex count = 0 in source, --refuted tokens reference semantic-crit). Existing assertions unchanged.

preserved_load_bearing_content: 'Refuted by Data' and 'Decisive' badge TEXT on kill-shot headings — unchanged; only chroma routed through semantic-crit tokens.
Silent Failures, Actual Failures, Acknowledged Failures stat-card TEXT and numerals — unchanged.
FAIL-NNN entry-card content (FAIL-001 through FAIL-004 and later) — unchanged; only border-left color neutralized.
td.v-refuted verdict cells and .vt-refuted / .vl-refuted pills — render red via re-routed tokens.
Pie-chart slice encoding (refuted = red) — visually unchanged in this EXP; future Phase B can re-render the SVG with var() fills.
Dark-theme preference — every new token reference has a dark override.
[SUSPENDED] inline tag TEXT — unchanged; only color neutralized to --ink-3.
out_of_scope_for_this_exp: Glyph + label pairing for verdict pills (EXP-A8 / Item 3 a11y).
Moving the accuracy-math 4+4 card to a sidebar (EXP-A6 / Item 4).
Typography application — body serif, chrome sans, data mono (EXP-A2 / Item 6 — drafted as EXP-207).
Re-rendering the pie-chart SVG with var() fills (Phase B).
Dropping the --semantic- prefix (post-A5 cleanup EXP, if approved).
notes_for_human_review_group: JUDGMENT CALL ON KILL-SHOT BADGES: The brief's rule is 'Red survives only inside table cells that flag an actual contradiction'. Strict reading says the 'Refuted by Data' / 'Decisive' pill-badges on kill-shot <h2> headings (part5.html) should be neutralized too — they are <span>s in prose, not <td>s. The analyst's default is to keep them red via var(--semantic-crit) because each kill-shot is a 'row' in the Kill Shots argumentative table and the badge IS a contradiction marker. If the group prefers the strict reading, the flip is a one-line swap of var(--semantic-crit) to var(--ink-2) across the six badge substrings in part5.html — cost is trivial post-tokenization.

PIE-CHART SVG IS NOT MIGRATED. The overview pie chart contains raw red hex literals (#E57373, #ef5350, #B71C1C) baked into pre-rendered SVG. These are data-viz fills, not chrome — the refuted slice IS refuted — but they are the most visible red on the page. Scope-of-work: migrating the SVG requires touching the pie-chart emitter function and adds complexity this EXP is trying to avoid. Flagging as a Phase B follow-up. If the group wants it in A5, a follow-on EXP-A5b can ship without breaking A5's landing.

CA-TAG.TAG-MONITORING-HARDCODED moved to --semantic-warn (amber), not --semantic-crit. Rationale: its semantic is 'warning, hardcoded number, watch out' — not 'refuted'. The other three .ca-tag variants (tag-monitoring-live, tag-monitoring-none, tag-pending) stay at their current chroma (greens, neutrals, faint-grey) — none is red, so no migration needed for them. If the group wants a consistency pass across all .ca-tag variants (re-route through semantic tokens even where the chroma is unchanged), that is a follow-up cleanup.

STRICT-RED TEST POSSIBILITY: Consider adding a CI check that greps for raw red hex across generate-html.js and sections.json and fails the build on any match outside a whitelist (pie-chart SVG block, verdict token declarations in the :root block, and the semantic-crit declarations themselves). This EXP adds a test.js assertion that covers a subset; a sharper check could be a build-step lint. Flagging for consideration.

VERIFICATION SCRIPT: Recommend the human review group asks the decider to generate a color-usage audit after integration — a small Node script that inventories every hex/rgb occurrence in docs/index.html by selector/tag and buckets by role. This makes the brief's 'red pixels < 5%' acceptance criterion concretely measurable. Not in this EXP's scope but flagging as an obvious companion artifact.

BATCH APPROVAL OPTION: A5 lands cleanly on top of A1 (EXP-202) alone; it does NOT strictly require A2 (typography). The human group could approve EXP-202 + EXP-210 as a batch without EXP-207 landing first, if the operator wants palette restriction to ship before typography migration. The EXP's integration_blocked_until clause captures the A1 ordering constraint but not an A2 one.
cross_references: HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001 (monitor/analyst/human-notes.json) — source brief; Item 7 (restrict red) and NC3 (Kill Shots label preserved).
monitor/analyst/design-review-2026-04-17-external.html — external review source document; Finding F2 motivates this EXP.

EXP-202 (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-202.json) — supplies --semantic-crit / --semantic-crit-soft / --semantic-warn / --semantic-warn-soft / --ink-3 / --rule tokens this EXP consumes. Must integrate before or with this EXP.
EXP-207 (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-207.json) — EXP-A2 typography application. Independent of this EXP; may integrate in either order.
EXP-208 / EXP-209 — EXP-A3 stance statement and EXP-A4 bar chart. No coupling to this EXP.
Integrity findings on hardcoded light colors (#f5f5f5 WGS84 block, #fff Tesla card) — EXP-A8 scope, not absorbed here.
newsworthiness_caveat: Palette restriction on its own is not newsworthy — no verdict change, no claim change, no new evidence. The full Phase A landing (A1 through A8 approved and integrated) may be newsworthy as a credibility-through-restraint design refresh. Reserve the headline for the Phase A completion EXP, not EXP-A5.

completion_signal.action: This EXP is DRAFT only. The signal to the decider is: 'Draft is ready; do NOT integrate until HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN lands with approved_exp_ids containing EXP-210.' The decider must leave this EXP in the tracker with status=complete but must not route it into the normal integration flow until the approval HNOTE arrives. Also requires EXP-202 to be integrated before EXP-210 is eligible for integration (ordering constraint captured in integration_blocked_until).

tldr_evidence: Phase A design refresh Item 7: restrict red to in-table contradictions. This EXP re-routes the --refuted / --refuted-solid verdict tokens through EXP-202's --semantic-crit tokens, neutralizes seven stat-card and entry-card border-left red accents (#c45050, #8b0000) to var(--rule), migrates six kill-shot inline pill-badges from raw hex to semantic-crit tokens, and tokenizes one claim-assessment tag that should be amber (warn) rather than red. Kill Shots tab and label preserved. Pie-chart SVG left untouched as a data-viz case flagged for Phase B.
tldr_verdict: DRAFT — palette restriction only, no content change. Surviving red lives in verdict pills and kill-shot contradiction badges (both routed through --semantic-crit). Seven stat-card border-lefts, one [SUSPENDED] prose tag, and the tag-monitoring-hardcoded pill all move to neutral or warn tokens. Integration blocked until operator posts approval HNOTE naming EXP-210, and requires EXP-202 (tokens) to land first.
